title: asclepias aurea
intro: Asclepias aurea, also known as the golden ragwort or orange milkweed, is a perennial plant that belongs to the milkweed family and is native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Asclepias aurea include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, which can help prevent infections. Asclepias aurea can be prepared as an herbal remedy through tinctures, infusions, or salves, which can be used topically to treat skin issues or internally to support overall health and wellness.
